$schema_note: Combined verified cross-KG joins for the Proto-OKN FRINK federation. Merges the A-I clusters with the spoke-okn geolocation crosswalks: cluster J (ZIP, on spoke-okn's full ~42,724-ZIP set) and cluster K (county/state FIPS, including spoke-okn's attachment to the SAWGraph spatial hub). Duplicates removed. Counts are exact COUNT(DISTINCT) over the named graphs on the verified_on date. A consumer keyed by KG shortname should surface, for a given shortname, every entry where it appears as left_kg, right_kg, or bridge_kg. Clusters: (A) disease/phenotype - oard-kg is the MONDO/HP hub joining prokn, rdkg, nde and ubergraph; spoke-okn attaches via its 180 DOID Disease nodes (DIRECTLY to the DOID carriers prokn/biomarkerkg/nde, A7-A9, and through an ubergraph DOID->MONDO bridge to rdkg/oard-kg, A10-A11), (B) CAS chemical, (C) gene cluster - keyed across THREE id systems: Entrez/NCBI Gene (spoke-okn, spoke-genelab, rdkg, biobricks-aopwiki; in three IRI forms - see C_gene_cluster note - joined by bare-id rebuild; C4-C8), Ensembl (gene-expression-atlas-okn, pankgraph, spoke-okn's schema/ensembl crosswalk, AOP-Wiki; C1-C3, F1-F2), and HGNC (prokn, bridged to Entrez through wikidata P354->P351; C9), (D) SAWGraph S2 spatial hub, (E) toxicology/AOP CAS, (F) AOP-Wiki gene bridge, (G) UniProt protein, (H) soil-carbon spatial leaf, (I) manufacturing supply-chain, (J) spoke-okn ZIP geography, (K) spoke-okn county/state-FIPS geography.
merged_from: gene-crosswalks-extension.json (C5-C9: rdkg & AOP-Wiki Entrez routes, prokn HGNC via wikidata)
disease-crosswalks (A7-A11: spoke-okn DOID to prokn/biomarkerkg/nde direct, to rdkg/oard-kg via ubergraph DOID->MONDO)

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: AOP-Wiki stressor chemicals are https://identifiers.org/cas/ (https); the toxcast/ice/tox21 cluster is http://identifiers.org/cas/ (http). Rewrite at query time: BIND(IRI(REPLACE(STR(?cas),'https://identifiers.org/cas/','http://identifiers.org/cas/')) AS ?c2), then join on ?c2.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Chemical stressors of Adverse Outcome Pathways (AOP-Wiki) that are also assayed in ToxCast / Tox21 / ICE.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same https->http Ensembl rewrite as F1. GXA Ensembl genes are node IRIs (http://identifiers.org/ensembl/), so the rewritten AOP-Wiki id joins directly on GXA's subject.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Baseline tissue expression (GXA) for genes implicated as AOP key-event targets (AOP-Wiki).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Three UniProt IRI forms exist across the protein KGs. AOP-Wiki proteins are own-node IRIs https://identifiers.org/uniprot/{ACC}; ProKN proteins (up:Protein) are http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/{ACC}. Extract the bare accession (REPLACE(STR(?u),'^.*/','')) and rebuild the target form: IRI(CONCAT('http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/', ?acc)). Note: do NOT additionally constrain the ProKN side with 'a up:Protein' in the join (some shared accessions ride other ProKN classes); match on IRI existence.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Proteins acting as AOP molecular targets (AOP-Wiki) with their ProKN protein-centric annotations (domains, PTMs, marker genes, disease links).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Both sides are the ncbigene form; only https (aopwiki) vs http (rdkg) differs. Rewrite https->http (or strip to bare id and rebuild) and join. No numeric transform needed.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT Entrez genes shared between AOP-Wiki key-event targets and rdkg rare-disease genes.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: AOP key-event target genes (AOP-Wiki) that are also rare-disease genes (rdkg) - toxicology pathway meets rare-disease genetics.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: AOP-Wiki gene/key-event targets carry Entrez as skos:exactMatch -> https://identifiers.org/ncbigene/{id}. Strip to the bare id and rebuild spoke-genelab's http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/{id}. NOTE: a genuinely NEW connection - spoke-genelab is Entrez-only, so AOP-Wiki (joined to the gene cluster on Ensembl in F1/F2) could not reach spoke-genelab before; the Entrez route is what links them.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT Entrez genes shared (AOP-Wiki carries ~1,480 ncbigene exactMatch ids).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Gene targets of AOP molecular initiating / key events (AOP-Wiki) that have spaceflight/omics evidence in spoke-genelab.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: AOP-Wiki gene targets are skos:exactMatch -> https://identifiers.org/ensembl/{ENSG} (https); the Ensembl cluster (spoke-okn, GXA, pankgraph; base file C-series) uses http://identifiers.org/ensembl/ (http). Rewrite https->http and join.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Gene targets of AOP molecular initiating / key events (AOP-Wiki) with their spoke-okn network/disease/compound associations.
ToxCast-screened chemicals (CAS) with Integrated Chemical Environment safety/curation data.
Tox21-screened chemicals (CAS) tied to Integrated Chemical Environment safety/curation data.

verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: tox21 keys chemicals as its OWN node IRIs (http://identifiers.org/cas/), exactly the form toxcast/ice carry as the object of edam:has_identifier (base file B1). tox21 therefore joins the existing CAS cluster directly, no rewrite. tox21 carries 8947 distinct CAS-typed subjects total.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Tox21 high-throughput screening hits (CAS) for chemicals also covered by EPA ToxCast assays.

verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: biomarkerkg keys disease on DOID, not MONDO; ProKN carries both and is the hinge into the MONDO side. To reach MONDO graphs, route DOID->MONDO through ubergraph oboInOwl:hasDbXref / skos:exactMatch.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Literature biomarkers for a disease (BiomarkerKG) cross-referenced to protein/marker-gene evidence (ProKN).
Baseline tissue expression (GXA) for any spoke-okn gene plus its network/disease/compound associations.

verified_crosswalks.note: 3-way intersection equals the GXA-spoke 2-way because pankgraph carries the full gene catalog.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: One-query gene dossier: expression (GXA) + associations (spoke-okn) + islet/pancreatic context (pankgraph).
Place Illinois wells/streams (hydrologykg) on the national S2 grid and roll up to county via spatialkg admin regions.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: NCI-PID proteins reference UniProt via owl:sameAs -> http://identifiers.org/uniprot/{ACC}; ProKN uses http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/{ACC}. Extract accession and rebuild the purl.uniprot.org form, then join on IRI existence.
verified_crosswalks.thin_thread_note: Small join: NCI-PID exposes only ~85 distinct UniProt ids via owl:sameAs (it is a focused signaling-pathway graph), of which 12 are present in ProKN. Real but low-cardinality; useful for pathway-context lookups, not bulk joins. NCI-PID also encodes some proteins as obo/PR_ terms that this CURIE route does not reach.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Signaling-pathway interaction proteins (NCI-PID) enriched with ProKN protein annotations.
NIAID datasets for a disease (NDE) plus its EHR phenotype signature (OARD).
Phenotypes clinically associated with rare diseases (OARD) that also have protein/marker-gene evidence (ProKN).
Diseases with both EHR phenotype associations (OARD) and marker-gene/protein evidence (ProKN).
Diabetes/pancreas disease-associated genes (pankgraph) with their spoke-okn associations.

verified_crosswalks.left_predicate: rdf:subject / rdf:object / rdfs:seeAlso / HAS_MARKER_GENE / biolink:target_for (gene as HGNC IRI)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: prokn keys genes ONLY on HGNC (no Entrez/Ensembl of its own), so the join goes through Wikidata: strip prokn's HGNC IRI to the bare numeric ?hnum; match a Wikidata gene item by wdt:P354 (HGNC ID) = ?hnum (bare-string literal); read that item's wdt:P351 (NCBI/Entrez gene ID) literal ?entrez; rebuild http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/{entrez} and join spoke-okn. identifier-mappings does NOT carry HGNC; wikidata is the bridge. ~99% of prokn HGNC ids resolve to a Wikidata P354 item (e.g. 188/189 on biolink:target_for).
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT spoke-okn Entrez genes reached from prokn's HGNC gene set via the Wikidata P354->P351 bridge (16,298 of spoke-okn's 16,346 genes). The same bridge reaches spoke-genelab (27,226) and, by the shared Entrez key, rdkg and biobricks-aopwiki (C5-C8). prokn carries ~38,328 distinct HGNC ids; most ride on rdf:subject/rdf:object (reified INDRA-evidence statements) and rdfs:seeAlso, not the direct biolink relations, so those predicates must be included to capture the full set. prokn also appears in the protein cluster (G1/G2) via UniProt - it carries marker genes as HGNC and proteins as UniProt.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Protein marker genes for a cell type / tissue (ProKN HAS_MARKER_GENE, HGNC) mapped via Wikidata to their spoke-okn network/disease/compound associations.
Rare-disease EHR phenotype profile (OARD) combined with contraindicated/treating drugs (RDKG, DrugBank).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: As C5: strip rdkg's 'ncbigene/' prefix to the bare id, rebuild spoke-genelab's http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/{id} node IRI, join on the rebuilt term.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT Entrez genes shared (of rdkg's 9,326 genes) - near-complete containment of rdkg's gene set in spoke-genelab.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Rare-disease genes (rdkg) with their spaceflight/omics observations in spoke-genelab (NASA GeneLab).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same Entrez gene, different IRI form. Strip rdkg's node IRI to the bare id (up to and including 'ncbigene/') and rebuild spoke-okn's form: BIND(IRI(CONCAT('http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/', ?id))); join on the rebuilt term. Both sides type the gene as biolink:Gene.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT Entrez genes shared (of rdkg's 9,326 biolink:Gene nodes); spoke-okn has 16,346 genes so the join is bounded by rdkg.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Rare-disease gene associations (rdkg: gene_associated_with_condition / has_gene) enriched with that gene's network/disease/compound associations in spoke-okn.
Aggregate Maine PFAS samples (sawgraph) to S2 cells and counties (spatialkg).

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Inline clade expansion: ?taxon rdfs:subClassOf* <parent NCBITaxon term>.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Expand a clade in ubergraph, pull SAWGraph contaminant-sampled species within it.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Both graphs classify manufacturers with the SAME predicate (SUDOKN:hasPrimaryNAICSClassifier) but mint the NAICS classifier IRI differently. securechainkg: 'http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/naics-311111.0-inst' (lowercase 'naics-', a trailing '.0', suffix '-inst'). sudokn: 'http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/NAICS%20332710-individual' (uppercase 'NAICS', %20 space, suffix '-individual'). A direct IRI join returns 0; extract the bare numeric code from each and join on it: securechainkg REPLACE(STR(?n),'^.*/naics-([0-9]+)\\.0-inst$','$1'); sudokn REPLACE(STR(?n),'^.*/NAICS%20([0-9]+)-individual$','$1').
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: The two KGs do NOT share manufacturer/company instance IRIs (securechainkg mints '...{name}-company-inst' from DHS/D&B/MSKG sources; sudokn mints '...{domain}-company-instance'); a direct company join is 0. They join on the industry-classification keys, not on individual firms. 35 of sudokn's 66 NAICS codes are also used in securechainkg (which spans 301 codes total).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: For a manufacturing NAICS industry, list SUDOKN small/medium manufacturers (capabilities, certificates, capacity) and SecureChain supply-chain participants (software/hardware products and their vulnerabilities) in that same industry.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: suppliesToIndustry targets a small shared SUDOKN industry-sector controlled vocabulary, again minted differently per graph. securechainkg: 'http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/renewableenergy-inst' (lowercase, no spaces, '-inst'). sudokn: 'http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/Renewable%20Energy-industry-individual' (Title Case, %20 spaces, '-industry-individual'). Direct IRI join is 0. Normalize each to a lowercase, space-free label and join on it: securechainkg LCASE(REPLACE(REPLACE(STR(?ind),'^.*/',''),'-inst$','')); sudokn LCASE(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(STR(?ind),'^.*/',''),'-industry-individual$',''),'%20','')).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Cross-reference the industry sectors served by SUDOKN manufacturers with the industry sectors of SecureChain supply-chain participants (e.g. which suppliers to the aerospace or automotive sector appear in both graphs).

verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: sockg is a THIRD spatial leaf on the SAWGraph S2 hub, alongside sawgraph (Maine PFAS, D2) and hydrologykg (Illinois wells/streams, D1). It materializes its S2 Level-13 cells as its own typed nodes in the identical KWG resource IRI form (http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/s2.level13.{id}), so the join to spatialkg's S2Cell_Level13 nodes is direct, no rewrite. All 1069 sockg S2 cells are present in spatialkg. Soil-carbon experiment sites are multi-state, so this leaf does not co-locate with the Maine/Illinois leaves; like them it joins via the hub, not leaf-to-leaf.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Place SOCKG soil-organic-carbon experiment sites on the national S2 grid (spatialkg) and roll them up to county/state admin regions.

verified_crosswalks.secondary_admin_levels: sockg also carries 36 AdministrativeRegion_1 (state) and 2 AdministrativeRegion_0 (country) nodes in the same KWG IRI form; the county level (62) is the most specific shared rollup key.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Aggregate SOCKG soil-carbon / GHG-flux measurements to the county (FIPS) level and join them to spatialkg's admin-region hierarchy for state-level summaries.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: None needed: both KGs mint gene nodes as the IDENTICAL non-standard Entrez (NCBI Gene) IRI http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/{id}. Join directly on the shared node IRI term (bind the same variable in both GRAPH blocks; do not FILTER(STR(?a)=?b)).
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT Entrez gene nodes common to both graphs (of spoke-okn's 16,346 biolink:Gene nodes, 16,326 are present in spoke-genelab). spoke-genelab carries the same Entrez IRIs.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: GeneLab spaceflight/omics gene observations (spoke-genelab) joined to that gene's network/disease/compound associations in spoke-okn, directly on the shared Entrez gene id.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: ubergraph CHEBI term -> hasDbXref CURIE 'cas:NNNN-NN-N' (lowercase). Rebuild biobricks IRI: IRI(CONCAT('http://identifiers.org/cas/', SUBSTR(STR(?casCurie),5))). CHEBI is canonical obo/CHEBI_ on both spoke-okn and ubergraph, no rewrite there.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Tie a ToxCast chemical (CAS) to spoke-okn gene/pathway/disease associations for the same compound via CHEBI.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn diseases are biolink:Disease nodes whose IRI IS the DOID term (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/DOID_{id}); biomarkerkg carries the same obo/DOID_ IRI. Join directly on the shared node IRI (bind the same term in both GRAPH blocks).
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT DOID diseases shared (of spoke-okn's 180). Pairs with the existing biomarkerkg<->prokn DOID join (A5).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Biomarkers for a disease (BiomarkerKB) alongside that disease's spoke-okn gene/compound associations, joined on DOID.

verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: bare 5-digit U.S. ZIP code, xsd:string literal (no IRI namespace)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same shared-variable ZIP-literal join (cross-graph STR()=FILTER returns 0). dreamkg uses http://schema.org/postalCode (http) vs ruralkg's https; the literal VALUES join regardless of predicate scheme.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: ALL 53 of dreamkg's (Philadelphia) ZIPs are present in spoke-okn. Small because dreamkg is a single-city homelessness-services graph; spoke-okn's national ZIP set fully contains it.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: For Philadelphia ZIPs, list DREAM-KG homelessness/social services in a ZIP alongside spoke-okn's local context.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn diseases are biolink:Disease nodes whose IRI IS the DOID term (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/DOID_{id}); nde carries the same obo/DOID_ IRI. Join directly on the shared node IRI (bind the same term in both GRAPH blocks). nde's schema:healthCondition carries DOID alongside MONDO/HP/NCIT; this join uses its DOID values.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT DOID diseases shared (of spoke-okn's 180). nde also joins oard-kg on MONDO (A4); the DOID slice is the spoke-okn-reachable subset.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Infectious/immune-mediated disease datasets (NDE) for a disease, with that disease's spoke-okn associations, joined on DOID.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn keys diseases on DOID (obo/DOID_), oard-kg on MONDO (obo/MONDO_). Bridge through ubergraph: ?mondo skos:exactMatch ?doid (MONDO term exact-matches the spoke-okn DOID IRI), then match ?mondo in oard-kg. The oboInOwl:hasDbXref CURIE route ('DOID:{id}') gives identical coverage. spoke-okn has 180 DOID diseases.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT spoke-okn DOID diseases mapped to a MONDO present in oard-kg (exactMatch and hasDbXref routes both give 40). Attaches spoke-okn to the disease-cluster hub.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: EHR-derived disease-phenotype associations (OARD, MONDO) for a spoke-okn disease (DOID), bridged through ubergraph.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn diseases are biolink:Disease nodes whose IRI IS the DOID term (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/DOID_{id}); prokn carries the same obo/DOID_ IRI. Join directly on the shared node IRI (bind the same term in both GRAPH blocks).
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT DOID diseases shared (of spoke-okn's 180 biolink:Disease nodes).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: spoke-okn disease associations (genes/compounds/prevalence/mortality) for diseases ProKN also describes, joined on DOID.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn keys diseases on DOID (obo/DOID_), rdkg on MONDO (obo/MONDO_). Bridge through ubergraph: ?mondo skos:exactMatch ?doid (MONDO term exact-matches the spoke-okn DOID IRI), then match ?mondo in rdkg. The oboInOwl:hasDbXref CURIE route ('DOID:{id}') gives identical coverage. spoke-okn has 180 DOID diseases.
verified_crosswalks.verified_count_note: DISTINCT spoke-okn DOID diseases whose ubergraph-equivalent MONDO term is present in rdkg (149 of 180) - rdkg is rare-disease-rich, the broadest spoke-okn disease join.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Rare-disease gene/drug associations (rdkg, MONDO) for a spoke-okn disease (DOID), bridged through ubergraph DOID->MONDO.

verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: bare 5-digit U.S. ZIP code, xsd:string literal (no IRI namespace)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same shared-variable ZIP-literal join as J1. ruralkg ENDPOINT QUIRK: binding the predicate directly as <https://schema.org/postalCode> returns 0 even though the triples exist; match it with a variable plus FILTER(CONTAINS(STR(?p),'postalCode')). ruralkg ZIPs are clean 5-digit xsd:string.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: ALL 4,938 of ruralkg's ZIPs are present in spoke-okn (full containment). Separately ruralkg carries censusCounty as KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS}, so it also meets spoke-okn at the county level (see K2) and bridges to the spatialkg hub.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Combine spoke-okn ZIP-level context with ruralkg rural health/justice indicators for the same ZIP, and roll up to county via ruralkg's KWG censusCounty.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same FIPS-extract-and-rebuild as K1. ruralkg's censusCounty OBJECT is already a KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS} IRI, so the rebuilt spoke-okn IRI joins it directly (shared-variable binding). ruralkg is national, so it matches more spoke-okn counties than spatialkg does.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: All 3,196 spoke-okn county nodes are present in ruralkg's censusCounty set; ruralkg's national county coverage fully contains spoke-okn's. ruralkg thus bridges spoke-okn county data to its rural health/justice indicators and (via the shared KWG county form) onward to the spatialkg hub.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Join spoke-okn county-level environmental/health rollups with ruralkg rural-resilience (health + justice) indicators for the same county FIPS.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same FIPS-extract-and-rebuild as K1. sockg counties are KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS} nodes (base-file H2), so the rebuilt spoke-okn IRI joins directly.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: 31 of sockg's ~62 soil-carbon experiment counties coincide with spoke-okn counties. Small but real; lets soil-organic-carbon / GHG-flux county context (sockg) meet spoke-okn's environmental-health county data on the same KWG county key both share with the hub.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: For counties with SOCKG soil-carbon experiment sites, pull spoke-okn's county-level environmental/health context.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Same 2-digit-FIPS extract-and-rebuild as K4; join to sockg's AdministrativeRegion_1 state nodes (KWG form, base-file H2).
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: 18 states are common to spoke-okn and sockg's multi-state soil-carbon footprint. State-level coincidence key between the two.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Compare spoke-okn state-level environmental/health summaries with the states where SOCKG runs soil-carbon experiments.

verified_crosswalks.left_predicate: node-iri (.../location/{FIPS5}; a schema:AdministrativeArea county node, label '... County/Parish/Borough')
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn county nodes carry the 5-digit county FIPS as the node-IRI suffix (https://purl.org/okn/frink/kg/spoke-okn/location/01001), NOT as a KWG IRI. spatialkg counties are KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS} nodes. Extract the bare FIPS from the spoke-okn IRI (REPLACE(STR(?c),'^.*/location/([0-9]{5})$','$1')) and rebuild the KWG IRI (IRI(CONCAT('http://stko-kwg.geog.ucsb.edu/lod/resource/administrativeRegion.USA.',?fips))), then use that rebuilt IRI as the SAME variable in the spatialkg GRAPH block. FRINK GOTCHA: a cross-graph 'FILTER(?a=?b)'/'FILTER(STR(?a)=?b)' over two GRAPH blocks silently returns 0; bind the same term in both blocks instead.
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: THIS IS spoke-okn's attachment to the SAWGraph spatial hub. (It supersedes the earlier 'spoke-okn does not attach to spatialkg' non-join, which was assessed only at the ZIP/ZipCodeArea level: spoke-okn has no KWG zip node and no S2 cell, but it DOES carry county FIPS in its node IRIs.) 3,122 of spoke-okn's 3,196 county nodes match a spatialkg county (spatialkg covers the 48 contiguous states + DC; the ~74 unmatched are AK/HI/PR/territory counties spatialkg does not load). Via this key spoke-okn rolls up to spatialkg's S2 grid and AdministrativeRegion hierarchy.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Aggregate spoke-okn ZIP-level environmental/health data to the county, then place those counties on the spatialkg S2 grid / admin-region hierarchy and pull neighboring counties or state rollups.

verified_crosswalks.left_predicate: node-iri (.../location/{FIPS2}; a schema:AdministrativeArea state node, label = state name)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: spoke-okn state nodes carry the 2-digit state FIPS as the node-IRI suffix (.../location/22 = Louisiana). Extract it (REPLACE(STR(?s),'^.*/location/([0-9]{2})$','$1')) and rebuild the KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS} IRI; join to spatialkg's AdministrativeRegion_1 state nodes on that rebuilt IRI (shared variable).
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: 51 of spoke-okn's 52 state nodes (50 states + DC; spoke-okn additionally carries PR/territory) match spatialkg states. Coarse but complete state-level rollup key. Note spoke-okn ALSO carries a 2-letter postal state code (schema:state, e.g. 'FL') on its ZIP nodes; the 2-digit FIPS node IRI is the cleaner key for joining the KWG stack.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: Roll spoke-okn data up to the state and join state-level summaries to the spatialkg admin-region hierarchy.

verified_crosswalks.key_namespace: bare 5-digit U.S. ZIP code, xsd:string literal (no IRI namespace)
verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Join on the ZIP xsd:string literal. spoke-okn: the ZIP is the rdfs:label of EVERY .../location/{STATE}-{ZIP} node (~42,724 of them); identify zip nodes by the IRI pattern location/[A-Z]{2}-[0-9]+ (do NOT gate on rdfs:seeAlso 'ZipCodeArea' - only 978 nodes carry that, and it is an unusable HTML-anchor literal anyway). sudokn: SUDOKN:hasZipcodeValue mixes U.S. ZIPs with Canadian postal codes (e.g. 'M3C 1T5'); constrain to U.S. with FILTER(REGEX(STR(?z),'^[0-9]{5}$')) if needed. FRINK GOTCHA: a cross-graph 'FILTER(STR(?a)=?b)' over two GRAPH blocks silently returns 0; bind the SAME variable term in both GRAPH blocks (both are xsd:string).
verified_crosswalks.hinge_note: Joins on ZIP literals, not company/location IRIs. 12,329 of sudokn's ~12,914 U.S. ZIPs coincide with a spoke-okn ZIP (spoke-okn is a near-complete national ZIP reference, so the join is bounded by sudokn's coverage).
verified_crosswalks.example_question: For a ZIP, list the SUDOKN small/medium manufacturers there (NAICS, capabilities, certificates) alongside spoke-okn's local environmental/health context.

verified_crosswalks.iri_normalization: Inline category expansion: ?disease rdfs:subClassOf* <parent MONDO term>; join expanded set to target KG in same query.
verified_crosswalks.example_question: All cardiovascular-disease subtypes (not just the parent term) present in OARD with their phenotypes.

cluster_notes.A_disease: spoke-okn keys diseases on DOID (180 biolink:Disease nodes whose IRI is the obo/DOID_ term). It joins the DOID carriers DIRECTLY on the shared DOID IRI: prokn 96 (A7), biomarkerkg 79 (A8), nde 20 (A9; nde's schema:healthCondition holds DOID alongside MONDO/HP/NCIT). The MONDO-keyed members need a DOID->MONDO bridge through ubergraph (?mondo skos:exactMatch ?doid, equivalently oboInOwl:hasDbXref 'DOID:{id}' - both give identical coverage): rdkg 149 (A10, the broadest spoke-okn disease join since rdkg is rare-disease-rich), oard-kg 40 (A11, attaching spoke-okn to the disease-cluster hub; the existing DOID pair biomarkerkg<->prokn is A5). Every federation disease KG is thus reachable from spoke-okn: DOID carriers directly, MONDO members via ubergraph. Caveat: spoke-okn's disease set is small (180), so counts are bounded by spoke-okn.
cluster_notes.C_gene_cluster: The gene cluster spans THREE id systems. (1) ENTREZ / NCBI Gene is the federation's de-facto gene join key, but in THREE IRI FORMS: http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/{id} (spoke-okn and spoke-genelab gene NODES; spoke-okn types them biolink:Gene, 16,346 nodes), http://identifiers.org/ncbigene/{id} (rdkg gene nodes), and https://identifiers.org/ncbigene/{id} (biobricks-aopwiki, as skos:exactMatch on key-event target genes). Same numeric id - join by stripping to the bare integer and rebuilding the partner's form (bind the same term in both GRAPH blocks; never FILTER(STR(?a)=?b), which returns 0 here). Verified Entrez pairs: spoke-okn<->spoke-genelab (C4, 16,326), rdkg<->spoke-okn (C5, 8,475), rdkg<->spoke-genelab (C6, 9,034), aopwiki<->spoke-genelab (C7, 1,472), aopwiki<->rdkg (C8, 1,193). C7 is notable: spoke-genelab is Entrez-only, so AOP-Wiki - previously tied to the cluster only on Ensembl (F1/F2) - reaches it only via this Entrez route. (2) ENSEMBL members are gene-expression-atlas-okn, pankgraph and biobricks-aopwiki, plus spoke-okn via its schema/ensembl crosswalk (16,316 of its 16,346 genes carry an http://identifiers.org/ensembl/{ENSG} value). pankgraph is Ensembl-ONLY (its gene nodes are ensembl IRIs with no Entrez xref; only expressed_in->CL and functional_association->GO edges). The C1-C3/F1 joins to spoke-okn remain valid and unchanged, but the spoke-okn side is reached via the schema/ensembl predicate (Ensembl as object), not the gene node. (3) HGNC is carried ONLY by prokn (http://identifiers.org/hgnc/{N}, ~38,328 distinct ids, mostly on rdf:subject/rdf:object reified INDRA statements and rdfs:seeAlso). prokn has no Entrez/Ensembl of its own, so it bridges to the Entrez world through the WIKIDATA graph: HGNC bare id -> wdt:P354 -> same item's wdt:P351 (Entrez literal) -> rebuild the spoke Entrez IRI (C9: prokn<->spoke-okn 16,298, prokn<->spoke-genelab 27,226; ~99% of prokn HGNC ids resolve via P354). Note identifier-mappings does NOT carry HGNC - wikidata is the bridge. prokn is also a protein-cluster member (G1/G2, UniProt): it holds marker genes as HGNC and proteins as UniProt. HINGES: spoke-okn ties the Entrez and Ensembl sub-worlds together (it alone carries both - Entrez node + Ensembl crosswalk); wikidata ties HGNC to Entrez. So every gene member is reachable from every other: the Entrez core (spoke-okn, spoke-genelab, rdkg, aopwiki) directly, the Ensembl members (GXA, pankgraph) via spoke-okn's crosswalk, and prokn (HGNC) via wikidata.
cluster_notes.E_toxicology_cas: biobricks-tox21 and biobricks-aopwiki both attach to the CAS chemical cluster anchored by biobricks-toxcast / biobricks-ice (base file B1). tox21 joins natively (same http://identifiers.org/cas/ node-IRI form); AOP-Wiki needs the https->http rewrite. This makes AOP-Wiki a hinge: its stressors carry CAS (chemical cluster) AND its key-event targets carry Ensembl + UniProt (gene/protein clusters).
cluster_notes.F_aopwiki_gene_bridge: AOP-Wiki's skos:exactMatch block carries genes as BOTH Entrez (~1,480 https://identifiers.org/ncbigene/ ids) and Ensembl (~1,478 https://identifiers.org/ensembl/ENSG ids), plus hmdb/chebi/pubchem.compound/drugbank chemical ids. F1/F2 use its Ensembl route (to spoke-okn and GXA); the Entrez route is now verified too and links the Entrez-only member spoke-genelab (C7) and rdkg (C8). Standard https->http (or bare-id rebuild) normalization applies.
cluster_notes.G_protein_cluster: UniProt accession is a federation-wide protein key but appears in at least three IRI forms: http://identifiers.org/uniprot/ (ncipidkg), https://identifiers.org/uniprot/ (aopwiki), and http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/ (prokn, which models proteins with the UniProt core ontology). Always join on the bare accession, never the raw IRI.
cluster_notes.H_soil_spatial: sockg (Soil Organic Carbon KG) joins the SAWGraph spatial hub (base D-series) as a third leaf next to sawgraph (Maine PFAS) and hydrologykg (Illinois). It reuses the SAWGraph/KWG spatial stack wholesale: S2Cell_Level13 cells, GADM AdministrativeRegion_0..2 nodes, kwg:sfWithin / sfContains, spatial-full:connectedTo, and GeoSPARQL geometries — all in native KWG IRI forms, so attachment to spatialkg needs no rewrite. Note: sockg ALSO carries contaminoso:casNumber (coso/contaminoso vocabulary) on its chemical entities, the same CAS chemical world as base-file B1 (toxcast/ice) and the E-series — a real thread into the chemical cluster that is intentionally NOT expanded here (out of scope: chemical/tox is already covered).
cluster_notes.I_manufacturing_supply_chain: securechainkg embeds the SUDOKN manufacturing ontology (http://asu.edu/semantics/SUDOKN/*) almost in full on its Manufacturer entities: hasPrimaryNAICSClassifier, suppliesToIndustry, hasProcessCapability, hasMaterialCapability, organizationLocatedIn, hasCertificate, hasNumberOfEmployees, hasOrganizationYearOfEstablishment. This makes SecureChain and SUDOKN siblings on the manufacturing side. They share the industry-classification vocabularies (NAICS codes I1; industry sectors I2) but NOT the individual company instances (different IRI minting), so joins are at the industry-class level. Beyond the manufacturing overlap, securechainkg adds a software-supply-chain layer with no cross-KG partner in the federation: Software / SoftwareVersion / Vulnerability (CVE-style ids via schema:identifier), Hardware / HardwareVersion (CPE 2.3 via secure-chain:cpe23), license (schema:license) and dependsOn / vulnerableTo edges. These are useful securechainkg-internal join keys but have no second graph to bridge to here.
cluster_notes.J_spoke_okn_zip_geography: spoke-okn carries ~42,724 ZIP nodes (.../location/{STATE}-{ZIP}), ALL with a bare-ZIP rdfs:label (xsd:string) - the clean cross-KG key. (Only 978 also have a KWG ZipCodeArea rdfs:seeAlso, stored as an HTML-anchor literal and unusable as an IRI; do not gate on it - that was the bug that undercounted the first version of this file.) Partners with bare 5-digit ZIPs join directly: sudokn (hasZipcodeValue, 12,329), ruralkg (schema:postalCode, 4,938 = all of ruralkg), dreamkg (schema:postalCode, 53 = all of dreamkg). For coarser geography spoke-okn also exposes county FIPS and state FIPS in its node IRIs - see the admin-region file (cluster K), including its attachment to the spatialkg hub. Pervasive failure mode: the FRINK cross-graph literal-join quirk (FILTER(STR(?a)=?b) yields 0; bind the same variable term in both GRAPH blocks).
cluster_notes.K_spoke_okn_admin_region_fips: spoke-okn's geographic hierarchy is fully FIPS-coded above the ZIP level, with the code living in the node-IRI suffix (.../location/{FIPS5} for counties, .../location/{FIPS2} for states) rather than in any KWG/identifiers.org IRI. Because the SAWGraph spatial stack (spatialkg hub; sockg and the sawgraph/hydrologykg leaves; ruralkg's censusCounty) keys admin regions on KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS}, the join is: extract the bare FIPS from spoke-okn -> rebuild the KWG IRI -> match as a shared-variable term. County FIPS is the strongest spoke-okn geographic key in the federation (national, 3,196 counties): spatialkg 3,122 (= spoke-okn's attachment to the hub), ruralkg 3,196 (full containment), sockg 31. State FIPS adds a coarse rollup (spatialkg 51, sockg 18). IMPORTANT CORRECTIONS to the prior files: (1) the earlier 'spoke-okn <-> spatialkg = 0 / no hub attachment' non-join was a ZIP-level result only; via county/state FIPS spoke-okn DOES attach to the hub (K1, K4). (2) The ZIP cluster (J) counts in spoke-okn-geolocation-crosswalks.json were gated on the 978 ZIP nodes that carry a KWG ZipCodeArea reference, but spoke-okn actually has ~42,724 ZIP nodes ALL bearing a bare-ZIP rdfs:label; recomputed on the full set the ZIP joins are far larger - sudokn 12,329 (was 387), ruralkg 4,938 (was 178), dreamkg 53 (was 4) - i.e. spoke-okn is effectively a complete national ZIP/county/state reference and the join is bounded by the PARTNER's coverage, not spoke-okn's. CITY level does not join: spoke-okn's ~67,909 city/place nodes are name slugs with no FIPS/GEOID (see known_non_joins).

rollup_keys: Census GEOID as Data Commons IRI: https://datacommons.org/browser/geoId/{geoid}
spatial_hub_note.topology: spatialkg is the national S2 grid + admin-region hub. sawgraph (Maine) and hydrologykg (Illinois) are different-state instances that attach to the hub via S2 cells; they do not overlap each other directly. spoke-okn also attaches to the hub at the county and state admin-region levels (national county coverage).
spatial_hub_note.spoke_okn_attachment: CONFIRMED: spoke-okn attaches to the spatialkg hub via county FIPS (3,122; K1) and state FIPS (51; K4), by rebuilding the KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS} IRI from spoke-okn's bare-FIPS node IRIs. It does NOT attach via ZIP (spatialkg has no ZipCodeArea nodes) or S2 cell (spoke-okn has none of its own). spoke-okn is now a THIRD admin-region-keyed member of the SAWGraph spatial world alongside sockg, sawgraph and hydrologykg - and the only one with national county coverage.

known_non_joins.diagnosis: SAWGraph owl:sameAs points to geoconnex.us/ref/pws/ reference IRIs that are NOT materialized as subjects in the geoconnex snapshot (tested http/https/reference. forms). Reference-IRI vs materialized-node mismatch; not fixable by rewrite. Do not retry.

identifier-mappings does not carry identifiers.org/cas/ or identifiers.org/pubchem.compound/ IRIs (bound lookups returned zero). Use the ubergraph CHEBI route (B2) to bridge the chemical clusters, not identifier-mappings/wikidata.
Named graph https://purl.org/okn/frink/kg/digcfdekg returns zero triples at this endpoint. Nothing to join.
fips:CountyCode is in the curated schema but unpopulated (returns zero). SCALES geography is custom IRIs / bare literals.

find_crosswalks returns empty: no mapping predicates, no ontology/identifier node IRIs, no domain-predicate ids. EvoWeb (co-evolving genes) encodes its gene/cluster entities as custom IRIs, so it shares no off-the-shelf join key with the gene/protein clusters. Would need a dedicated gene xref table. UNVERIFIED.

Urban Flooding OKN is large; the node-IRI / domain-predicate scan timed out even at sample=300000 and surfaced no standard crosswalk namespaces in the slice seen. No S2 / KWG admin-region / geoconnex join to the SAWGraph spatial hub was confirmable here. Re-profile with a smaller sample before assuming a spatial join exists.

Urban Flooding OKN shares 0 S2Cell_Level13 nodes with spatialkg (direct typed-node join). Despite being a spatial KG it does not attach to the SAWGraph S2 hub in the slice reachable here; it uses a different spatial encoding. Consistent with the base extension note (unprofiled-large). A FIPS/geometry route may exist but was not confirmable; re-profile with a narrow sample before assuming a spatial join.

0 shared S2Cell_Level13 nodes. (Geoconnex is also the target of a known base-file non-join from sawgraph on reference PWS IRIs.) Geoconnex's full node-IRI / crosswalk scan times out at this endpoint, so other hydrologic join keys (HUC, NHD, mainstem) were not exhaustively profiled — UNVERIFIED rather than proven absent.

0 shared S2Cell_Level13 nodes. The NASA GES DISC KG models datasets/instruments/platforms/science-keywords rather than gridded geography, so it does not attach to the S2 spatial hub. Any join would be via DOI (datasets) or GCMD science keywords, not the spatial hub — not pursued here (the natural DOI/keyword partners are publication/biomedical graphs).

0 shared S2Cell_Level13 nodes; no S2 hub attachment. (A taxon-based route to ubergraph/sawgraph in the spirit of base D3 might exist but was not explored — species/taxonomy is biology-adjacent and out of scope here.)

Climate Models KG is a self-contained custom ontology (climatepub4kg.github.io/ontology#*). Its geography is modeled as bespoke classes (City, Country, Country_Subdivision, Continent) with no KWG admin-region, S2, or FIPS IRIs, and 0 S2Cell_Level13 nodes shared with spatialkg. The only outward-facing handles are literal node properties — Variable.cf_standard_name (CF standard names) and Paper.id/title — neither an IRI join key. Effectively an island for IRI-level federation; a literal cf_standard_name match to NASA/GCMD keyword text is the only conceivable thin thread.

At the ZIP level spoke-okn does not reach spatialkg: spoke-okn references KWG ZipCodeArea only as HTML-anchor literals and spatialkg materializes no ZipCodeArea nodes (and spoke-okn has no S2 cell). NOTE: this is ZIP-level only - spoke-okn DOES attach to spatialkg via county FIPS (K1) and state FIPS (K4).

securechainkg embeds much of the SUDOKN ontology but does not populate SUDOKN:hasZipcodeValue in the reachable slice, so there is no ZIP-level location to join. Other location encodings were not confirmable (graph large, scans time out) - UNVERIFIED rather than proven absent.
Neighborhood Information KG exposes no zip/postal/FIPS/GEOID/tract predicate (find_crosswalks empty; targeted scan empty).
PHASES KG carries no zip/postal/FIPS predicate in the slice scanned.

spoke-okn's ~67,909 city/place nodes (.../location/{name-slug}, labels like 'Outside city limits') carry NO FIPS place code, GEOID, or other external identifier - only a name. A city-level join would have to be on the city NAME string, which is unreliable across the federation (same name in many states, inconsistent casing/abbreviation, 'Outside city limits' placeholders), so there is no clean key-based city crosswalk. Use the county (K1-K3) or state (K4-K5) FIPS level for reliable joins; drop to ZIP (cluster J) for finer geography.

A 150,000-triple profile of BiomarkerKB surfaced only DOID (disease), CHEBI (chemical) and CL (cell type) ids on its OBCI domain predicates - no standard gene IRIs. If biomarker->gene links exist they are not on an off-the-shelf gene id here; re-profile its biomarker/gene predicates specifically before assuming a gene join. (evoweb and digcfdekg, the other gene-adjacent KGs, are already listed above as non-joins.)

islands.note: Profiled and found to share no public join key for their domain entities (custom IRIs/literals). NOTE: ruralkg, dreamkg and sudokn were islands in the base file w.r.t. the biomedical/chemical clusters, but they DO join spoke-okn on geography (ZIP - cluster J; county FIPS - cluster K) and are no longer geographic islands. scales remains an island (its county-FIPS schema field is unpopulated); nikg exposes no geographic key at all.
thin_threads: ruralkg has 7 DrugBank drug IRIs (could touch rdkg DrugBank) and ~23 Wikidata links
ruralkg: censusCounty (KWG administrativeRegion.USA.{FIPS}) joins spoke-okn (K2) and the spatialkg hub; postalCode joins spoke-okn ZIPs (J2).
dreamkg: schema:postalCode joins spoke-okn on 53 Philadelphia ZIPs (J3).
sudokn: hasZipcodeValue joins spoke-okn on 12,329 ZIPs (J1).
